title: NETEC Hantavirus Resource Hub — Outbreak Timeline, Guidance Documents, Training
description: NETEC repository aggregating authoritative hantavirus resources for the 2026 outbreak: selected publications, outbreak timeline (MV Hondius → repatriation → NQU monitoring), PPE matrices, EMS donning/doffing procedures, and links to NETEC training webinars. Curated for HCW and EMS operational use.

title: VHF PPE Matrix — Final Updated 2024 (Applicable to Andes Virus)
description: NETEC VHF PPE selection matrix specifying minimum PPE by scenario: routine contact, AGPs, patient care, donning/doffing. Specifies AAMI Level 3/4 gown or coverall, fit-tested N95 or PAPR. Applicable to Andes virus given documented person-to-person transmission and airborne isolation requirement.

title: Hantavirus: Clinical Insights, Emerging Evidence, and What Every Healthcare Worker Should Know
description: Expert synthesis from Dr. Maria Frank (Johns Hopkins RESPTIC Director) and Dr. Steven Bradfute (UNM). Covers 'The Classic Five' lab indicators, poor prognostic markers, AAMI Level 3/4 gown + PAPR specification, and IPC guidance specific to Andes virus person-to-person transmission.

title: Nebraska Medicine / UNMC Asked to Monitor U.S. Repatriates — National Quarantine Unit Activation
description: Nebraska Medicine / UNMC National Quarantine Unit (NQU) — only federally funded quarantine unit in the US — activated to monitor repatriated MV Hondius passengers. 20-room unit with individual negative-pressure systems, all-volunteer staff with quarterly training, previously activated for Ebola (2014) and COVID (2020).

title: HAN 528 — Andes Virus Infection Associated with Cruise Ship Voyage
description: CDC Health Alert Network advisory for clinicians and public health officials. Includes case definitions, clinical guidance, HCW infection control precautions, and reporting requirements.

title: HAN Advisory #8 — Andes Strain Hantavirus
description: Recommends airborne infection isolation precautions for ALL suspected or confirmed Andes virus patient contacts. Includes EMS, hospital, and laboratory guidance specific to NYC providers.

title: Rapid Risk Assessment — Hantavirus Disease, Cruise Ship Cluster
description: ECDC assessment for EU/EEA countries. Risk for general population very low; risk for unprotected HCW contacts elevated. Provides PPE and IPC guidance.

title: Disease Outbreak News DON600 — Hantavirus Disease, Update
description: WHO update reporting expanded case counts and geographic spread across multiple countries. Investigations into exposure source and transmission dynamics ongoing.

title: Disease Outbreak News DON599 — Hantavirus Disease, Cruise Ship Cluster
description: Initial WHO DON confirming Andes virus (Orthohantavirus andesense) as causative agent following genomic sequencing at NICD South Africa and PCR at Geneva University Hospitals.

title: Laboratory Resources: Hantavirus — BSL-3, Centrifuge Safety, Specimen Shipping (UN2814)
description: NETEC laboratory quick reference (May 6, 2026): BSL-3 precautions for clinical specimens, biosafety cabinet requirement, avoid aerosol-generating procedures and cultures, centrifuge sealed rotor requirement, LRN contact for testing, UN2814 Category A shipping classification with certified-personnel requirement.

title: Hantavirus Training for Healthcare Providers
description: CDC online training module for HCPs covering hantavirus clinical recognition, infection control precautions, laboratory testing, and reporting requirements. Free CE/CME credit available.

title: EMS Model PPE Donning/Doffing — VHF Patient Without Active Bleeding/Vomiting/Diarrhea
description: NETEC step-by-step EMS donning and doffing sequence for VHF patients without active bleeding, vomiting, or diarrhea. Applicable to Andes virus EMS transport scenarios.

title: EMS Model PPE Donning/Doffing — VHF Patient With Active Bleeding/Vomiting/Diarrhea
description: NETEC step-by-step EMS donning and doffing sequence for VHF patients with active bleeding, vomiting, or diarrhea. Enhanced PPE requirements. Applicable to Andes virus EMS transport with symptomatic patients.
